items.front: Visual approach: definition and key characteristic per 7-4-1
items.back: ATC authorization for IFR aircraft to proceed visually and clear of clouds to the airport. NOT a standard instrument approach — has NO missed approach segment. Go-around handled as any go-around with IFR separation until lands or cancels IFR.

items.front: Go-around from visual approach at towered airport: two options per 7-4-1a
items.back: 1. Enter traffic pattern — no altitude assigned, pilot climbs to pattern altitude and is responsible for terrain/obstruction. 2. Proceed as instructed by ATC — ATC provides instructions to facilitate climb to minimum IFR altitude. Both require approved/visual separation from other IFR.

items.front: Go-around from visual approach at non-towered airport per 7-4-1b
items.back: Aircraft expected to complete landing ASAP or contact ATC for further clearance. ATC must maintain approved separation from other IFR aircraft.

items.front: Weather minima for visual approach vector per 7-4-2
items.back: Ceiling: at least 500 feet above the MVA/MIA. Visibility: 3 miles or greater. At airports without weather reporting: reasonable assurance that descent/flight to airport can be made visually and clear of clouds.

items.front: Vector for visual approach phraseology per 7-4-2
items.back: (Ident) FLY HEADING or TURN RIGHT/LEFT HEADING (degrees) VECTOR FOR VISUAL APPROACH TO (airport name). If no weather: add WEATHER NOT AVAILABLE.

items.front: Visual approach clearance: who may initiate per 7-4-3
items.back: Controllers may initiate, or pilots may request. Tower may clear when authorized by LOA with IFR facility or facility directive at collocated facilities. Pilot need not report airport/runway in sight (note).
Towered: '(Call sign) (control instructions) CLEARED VISUAL APPROACH RUNWAY (number).' Non-towered: '(Call sign) CLEARED VISUAL APPROACH TO (airport name).'

items.front: Approaches to multiple runways: key requirements per 7-4-4
items.back: Inform all aircraft (may use ATIS). Do not permit flight paths to intersect unless approved separation maintained. Intercept angle not greater than 30 degrees to extended centerline. Separate until higher aircraft has descended through lower aircraft's altitude.

1. Operating control tower. 2. Published name of CVFP and runway in clearance. 3. Ceiling at least 500 ft above MVA/MIA (or at/above MVA if following another aircraft). 4. Pilot affirms charted visual landmark or preceding aircraft in sight. USA/USN not applicable.

For authorized operators only (per LOA). Pilot must report airport in sight. Ceiling at least 500 ft above MVA/MIA, visibility 3 miles or greater. Phraseology: CLEARED RNAV VISUAL RUNWAY (number) APPROACH.

Pilot MUST request. Reported ground visibility at least 1 statute mile (or flight visibility 1 mile if ground not available). Pilot responsible for flight visibility, cloud clearance, terrain/obstruction clearance. May deviate from prescribed IFR pattern.
CLEARED CONTACT APPROACH. If required: AT OR BELOW (altitude) (routing). IF NOT POSSIBLE, (alternative procedures), AND ADVISE.
